# Models overview

Claude is a family of state-of-the-art large language models developed by Anthropic. This guide introduces the available models and compares their performance.

---

## Choosing a model

If you're unsure which model to use, start with **Claude Opus 4.8** for complex agentic coding and enterprise work. For workloads that need the highest available capability, use [Claude Fable 5](#claude-fable-5-and-claude-mythos-5).

All current Claude models support text and image input, text output, multilingual capabilities, and vision. Models are available through the Claude API, [Claude Platform on AWS](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-platform-on-aws), [Amazon Bedrock](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-in-amazon-bedrock), [Google Cloud](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-on-vertex-ai), and [Microsoft Foundry](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-in-microsoft-foundry).

Once you've picked a model, [learn how to make your first API call](/docs/en/get-started).

### Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5

Claude Fable 5 (`claude-fable-5`) is Anthropic's most capable widely released model. Claude Mythos 5 (`claude-mythos-5`) shares Claude Fable 5's specs and pricing and joins the invitation-only Claude Mythos Preview (`claude-mythos-preview`) within [Project Glasswing](https://anthropic.com/glasswing). See [Introducing Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5](/docs/en/about-claude/models/introducing-claude-fable-5-and-claude-mythos-5) for launch details and API changes.

Claude Fable 5 is generally available on the Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry beginning June 9, 2026. Claude Mythos 5 is not generally available: it is offered in limited availability to approved customers in [Project Glasswing](https://anthropic.com/glasswing), beginning the same day. For access, contact your Anthropic, AWS, or Google Cloud account team.

### Latest models comparison

| Feature                                                               | Claude Fable 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Claude Opus 4.8                                                                      | Claude Sonnet 5                                                                      | Claude Haiku 4.5                                                                       |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Description**                                                       | Next-generation intelligence for long-running agents                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | For complex agentic coding and enterprise work                                       | The best combination of speed and intelligence                                       | The fastest model with near-frontier intelligence                                      |
| **Claude API ID**                                                     | claude-fable-5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | claude-opus-4-8                                                                      | claude-sonnet-5                                                                      | claude-haiku-4-5-20251001                                                              |
| **Claude API alias**                                                  | claude-fable-5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | claude-opus-4-8                                                                      | claude-sonnet-5                                                                      | claude-haiku-4-5                                                                       |
| **AWS Bedrock ID**                                                    | anthropic.claude-fable-53                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | anthropic.claude-opus-4-83                                                           | anthropic.claude-sonnet-53                                                           | anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0                                               |
| **Google Cloud ID**                                                   | claude-fable-5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | claude-opus-4-8                                                                      | claude-sonnet-5                                                                      | claude-haiku-4-5\@20251001                                                             |
| **Pricing**1                                                          | $10 / input MTok $50 / output MTok                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | $5 / input MTok $25 / output MTok                                                    | $3 / input MTok $15 / output MTok4                                                   | $1 / input MTok $5 / output MTok                                                       |
| **[Extended thinking](/docs/en/build-with-claude/extended-thinking)** | No                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | No                                                                                   | No                                                                                   | Yes                                                                                    |
| **[Adaptive thinking](/docs/en/build-with-claude/adaptive-thinking)** | Yes (always on)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Yes                                                                                  | Yes                                                                                  | No                                                                                     |
| **Comparative latency**                                               | Slower                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Moderate                                                                             | Fast                                                                                 | Fastest                                                                                |
| **Context window**                                                    | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~555k words \ ~2.5M unicode characters. Claude Fable 5 uses the tokenizer introduced with Claude Opus 4.7; compared to models before Claude Opus 4.7, the same text produces roughly 30% more tokens. The exact increase depends on the content.">1M tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~555k words \ ~2.5M unicode characters">1M tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~555k words \ ~2.5M unicode characters">1M tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~150k words \ ~680k unicode characters">200k tokens</Tooltip> |
| **Max output**                                                        | 128k tokens                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | 128k tokens                                                                          | 128k tokens                                                                          | 64k tokens                                                                             |
| **Reliable knowledge cutoff**                                         | Jan 20262                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Jan 20262                                                                            | Jan 20262                                                                            | Feb 2025                                                                               |
| **Training data cutoff**                                              | Jan 2026                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Jan 2026                                                                             | Jan 2026                                                                             | Jul 2025                                                                               |

*1 - See [Pricing](/docs/en/about-claude/pricing) for complete pricing information including Batch API discounts and prompt caching rates.*

*2 - **Reliable knowledge cutoff** indicates the date through which a model's knowledge is most extensive and reliable. **Training data cutoff** is the broader date range of training data used. For more information, see [Anthropic's Transparency Hub](https://www.anthropic.com/transparency).*

*3 - Claude Fable 5, Claude Opus 4.8, and Claude Sonnet 5 are available on Bedrock through [Claude in Amazon Bedrock](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-in-amazon-bedrock) (the Messages-API Bedrock endpoint).*

*4 - Introductory pricing of $2 / $10 per MTok applies to Claude Sonnet 5 through August 31, 2026. See [Pricing](/docs/en/about-claude/pricing#claude-sonnet-5-introductory-pricing).*

<Info>
  Claude Mythos 5 and Claude Mythos Preview are offered separately for defensive cybersecurity workflows as part of [Project Glasswing](https://anthropic.com/glasswing). Access is invitation-only and there is no self-serve sign-up.
</Info>

<Note>
  Every Claude model ID is a pinned snapshot. Models with a date in the ID (for example, 

  `20250929`

  ) are fixed to that specific release. Starting with the Claude 4.6 generation, model IDs use a dateless format that is also a pinned snapshot, not an evergreen pointer. For models before the 4.6 generation, entries in the Claude API alias column are convenience pointers that resolve to a dated model ID. For details on the naming convention and how versioning works, see 

  [Model IDs and versioning](/docs/en/about-claude/models/model-ids-and-versions)

  .
</Note>

<Note>
  Starting with 

  **Claude Sonnet 4.5 and all subsequent models**

   (including Claude Sonnet 4.6), Bedrock offers two endpoint types: 

  **global endpoints**

   (dynamic routing for maximum availability) and 

  **regional endpoints**

   (guaranteed data routing through specific geographic regions). Google Cloud offers three endpoint types: global endpoints, 

  **multi-region endpoints**

   (dynamic routing within a geographic area), and regional endpoints. For more information, see 

  [Cloud platform pricing](/docs/en/about-claude/pricing#cloud-platform-pricing)

  .
</Note>

<Note>
  **Claude Platform on AWS**

   uses the same model IDs as the Claude API (for example, 

  `claude-opus-4-6`

  ), not Bedrock-style IDs. Model lifecycle on Claude Platform on AWS follows Anthropic's first-party 

  [Model deprecations](/docs/en/about-claude/model-deprecations)

  , not Bedrock's. See 

  [Available models](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-platform-on-aws#available-models)

   for the model list.
</Note>

<Tip>
  You can query model capabilities and token limits programmatically with the [Models API](/docs/en/api/models/list). The response includes `max_input_tokens`, `max_tokens`, and a `capabilities` object for every available model.
</Tip>

<Note>
  On Claude Opus 4.8, the `effort` parameter defaults to `high` on all surfaces, including the Claude API, Claude Code, and claude.ai. On Claude Sonnet 5, it defaults to `high` on the Claude API and Claude Code. Set `effort` explicitly to use a different level. See [Effort](/docs/en/build-with-claude/effort) for guidance on choosing a level.
</Note>

<Note>
  The Max output values above apply to the synchronous Messages API. On the [Message Batches API](/docs/en/build-with-claude/batch-processing#extended-output-beta), Claude Opus 4.8, Opus 4.7, Opus 4.6, Sonnet 5, and Sonnet 4.6 support up to 300k output tokens by using the `output-300k-2026-03-24` beta header.
</Note>

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Legacy models">
    The following models are still available. Consider migrating to current models for improved performance:

    | Feature                                                               | Claude Opus 4.7                                                                                                      | Claude Opus 4.6                                                                      | Claude Sonnet 4.6                                                                    | Claude Sonnet 4.5                                                                      | Claude Opus 4.5                                                                        | Claude Opus 4.1 (deprecated)                                                           |
    | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    | **Claude API ID**                                                     | claude-opus-4-7                                                                                                      | claude-opus-4-6                                                                      | claude-sonnet-4-6                                                                    | claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929                                                             | claude-opus-4-5-20251101                                                               | claude-opus-4-1-20250805                                                               |
    | **Claude API alias**                                                  | claude-opus-4-7                                                                                                      | claude-opus-4-6                                                                      | claude-sonnet-4-6                                                                    | claude-sonnet-4-5                                                                      | claude-opus-4-5                                                                        | claude-opus-4-1                                                                        |
    | **AWS Bedrock ID**                                                    | anthropic.claude-opus-4-76                                                                                           | anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1                                                         | anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6                                                          | anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0                                              | anthropic.claude-opus-4-5-20251101-v1:0                                                | anthropic.claude-opus-4-1-20250805-v1:0                                                |
    | **Google Cloud ID**                                                   | claude-opus-4-7                                                                                                      | claude-opus-4-6                                                                      | claude-sonnet-4-6                                                                    | claude-sonnet-4-5\@20250929                                                            | claude-opus-4-5\@20251101                                                              | claude-opus-4-1\@20250805                                                              |
    | **Pricing**                                                           | $5 / input MTok $25 / output MTok                                                                                    | $5 / input MTok $25 / output MTok                                                    | $3 / input MTok $15 / output MTok                                                    | $3 / input MTok $15 / output MTok                                                      | $5 / input MTok $25 / output MTok                                                      | $15 / input MTok $75 / output MTok                                                     |
    | **[Extended thinking](/docs/en/build-with-claude/extended-thinking)** | No                                                                                                                   | Yes                                                                                  | Yes                                                                                  | Yes                                                                                    | Yes                                                                                    | Yes                                                                                    |
    | **[Adaptive thinking](/docs/en/build-with-claude/adaptive-thinking)** | Yes                                                                                                                  | Yes                                                                                  | Yes                                                                                  | No                                                                                     | No                                                                                     | No                                                                                     |
    | **Comparative latency**                                               | Moderate                                                                                                             | Moderate                                                                             | Fast                                                                                 | Fast                                                                                   | Moderate                                                                               | Moderate                                                                               |
    | **Context window**                                                    | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~555k words \ ~2.5M unicode characters (Opus 4.7 uses a new tokenizer)">1M tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~750k words \ ~3.4M unicode characters">1M tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~750k words \ ~3.4M unicode characters">1M tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~150k words \ ~680k unicode characters">200k tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~150k words \ ~680k unicode characters">200k tokens</Tooltip> | <Tooltip tooltipContent="~150k words \ ~680k unicode characters">200k tokens</Tooltip> |
    | **Max output**                                                        | 128k tokens                                                                                                          | 128k tokens                                                                          | 128k tokens                                                                          | 64k tokens                                                                             | 64k tokens                                                                             | 32k tokens                                                                             |
    | **Reliable knowledge cutoff**                                         | Jan 20265                                                                                                            | May 20255                                                                            | Aug 20255                                                                            | Jan 20255                                                                              | May 20255                                                                              | Jan 20255                                                                              |
    | **Training data cutoff**                                              | Jan 2026                                                                                                             | Aug 2025                                                                             | Jan 2026                                                                             | Jul 2025                                                                               | Aug 2025                                                                               | Mar 2025                                                                               |

    <Warning>
      Claude Opus 4.1 (`claude-opus-4-1-20250805`) is deprecated and will be retired on August 5, 2026. Migrate to [Claude Opus 4.8](/docs/en/about-claude/models/migration-guide#migrating-from-claude-opus-47) before the retirement date.

      See [model deprecations](/docs/en/about-claude/model-deprecations) for details.
    </Warning>

    *5 - **Reliable knowledge cutoff** indicates the date through which a model's knowledge is most extensive and reliable. **Training data cutoff** is the broader date range of training data used.*

    *6 - Claude Opus 4.7 is available on Bedrock through [Claude in Amazon Bedrock](/docs/en/build-with-claude/claude-in-amazon-bedrock) (the Messages-API Bedrock endpoint).*
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

## Prompt and output performance

Claude 4 models excel in:

* **Performance:** Top-tier results in reasoning, coding, multilingual tasks, long-context handling, honesty, and image processing. See the [Claude 4 blog post](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-4) for more information.

* **Engaging responses:** Claude models are ideal for applications that require rich, human-like interactions.

  * If you prefer more concise responses, you can adjust your prompts to guide the model toward the desired output length. Refer to the [prompt engineering guides](/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering) for details.
  * For prompting best practices, see [Prompting best practices](/docs/en/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/claude-prompting-best-practices).

* **Output quality:** When migrating from previous model generations to Claude 4, you may notice larger improvements in overall performance.

## Migrating to Claude Opus 4.8

If you're currently using Claude Opus 4.7 or earlier Claude models, see [Migrating to Claude Opus 4.8](/docs/en/about-claude/models/migration-guide#migrating-from-claude-opus-47).

If you're currently using Claude Opus 4.6 or older Claude models, see [Migrating to Claude Opus 4.8 from Claude Opus 4.6](/docs/en/about-claude/models/migration-guide#migrating-from-claude-opus-46).

## Get started with Claude

If you're ready to start exploring what Claude can do for you, dive in! Whether you're a developer looking to integrate Claude into your applications or a user wanting to experience the power of AI firsthand, the following resources can help.

<Note>
  Looking to chat with Claude? Visit 

  [claude.ai](https://claude.ai)

  !
</Note>

<CardGroup cols={3}>
  <Card title="Intro to Claude" icon="check" href="/docs/en/intro">
    Explore Claude's capabilities and development flow.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Quickstart" icon="lightning" href="/docs/en/get-started">
    Learn how to make your first API call in minutes.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Claude Console" icon="code" href="/">
    Craft and test powerful prompts directly in your browser.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

If you have any questions or need assistance, don't hesitate to reach out to the [support team](https://support.claude.com/) or consult the [Discord community](https://www.anthropic.com/discord).
Assistant2026-07-10T17:06:54.446Z
Thinking
Assistant2026-07-10T17:06:56.913Z

Confirmed the cutoff for claude-sonnet-4-6:

  • Reliable knowledge cutoff: Aug 2025
  • Training data cutoff: Jan 2026

So to reliably force a search, the "recent" questions should target events after Aug 2025 — and the strongest forcers are outcomes after Jan 2026 (up to today, 2026-07-10). I designed the recent category around that.

Let me write the questions file (and, as you asked, add the judge prompt to prompts.py). I'm holding off on run_eval.py and any execution until you've verified the golds.

Assistant2026-07-10T17:07:59.155Z
📝 Write questions.json
/Users/souri/Downloads/Anthro Test/eval/questions.json
{
  "_meta": {
    "description": "Eval set for the Wikipedia-search QA agent. ~25 questions across 5 categories.",
    "target_model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
    "model_cutoffs": {
      "reliable_knowledge_cutoff": "2025-08",
      "training_data_cutoff": "2026-01",
      "note": "The 'recent' category targets events after the reliable cutoff (Aug 2025). Strongest search-forcers are outcomes after the training cutoff (Jan 2026), through today (2026-07-10)."
    },
    "field_schema": {
      "id": "unique id",
      "category": "single_hop | multi_hop | ambiguous_entity | unanswerable | recent",
      "question": "the user-facing question",
      "gold_answer": "reference answer; the LLM judge checks equivalence against this",
      "acceptable_answers": "list of normalized strings for programmatic closed-form match; [] when the answer isn't closed-form",
      "expected_behavior": "answer | disambiguate | refuse_or_correct",
      "expected_search": "true if a well-behaved agent should call search_wikipedia",
      "min_searches": "minimum distinct searches expected (grading is lenient; used as a signal, not a hard gate)",
      "verify": "true if the gold answer must be confirmed against Wikipedia before use (recent/volatile facts)",
      "notes": "grading hints / rationale"
    }
  },
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "single_01",
      "category": "single_hop",
      "question": "In what year was the Eiffel Tower completed?",
      "gold_answer": "1889.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["1889"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Completed March 1889 for the 1889 World's Fair."
    },
    {
      "id": "single_02",
      "category": "single_hop",
      "question": "What is the chemical symbol for gold?",
      "gold_answer": "Au.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["au"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "From Latin 'aurum'."
    },
    {
      "id": "single_03",
      "category": "single_hop",
      "question": "Who wrote the novel 'Pride and Prejudice'?",
      "gold_answer": "Jane Austen.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["jane austen", "austen"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Published 1813."
    },
    {
      "id": "single_04",
      "category": "single_hop",
      "question": "What is the capital city of Australia?",
      "gold_answer": "Canberra.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["canberra"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Common distractor is Sydney; correct answer is Canberra."
    },
    {
      "id": "single_05",
      "category": "single_hop",
      "question": "How many bones are in the adult human body?",
      "gold_answer": "206.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["206"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Standard count for a typical adult skeleton."
    },
    {
      "id": "multi_01",
      "category": "multi_hop",
      "question": "Which is taller, the Eiffel Tower or the Statue of Liberty, and by roughly how much?",
      "gold_answer": "The Eiffel Tower is much taller. It is about 330 m tall (to the tip, including antennas); the Statue of Liberty is about 93 m from ground to the tip of the torch. The Eiffel Tower is therefore roughly 235-240 m taller.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["eiffel tower", "eiffel"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 2,
      "verify": true,
      "notes": "Two lookups (both heights) + a comparison. Exact metres vary by what is measured (roof vs antenna; pedestal included or not) - judge on the 'Eiffel is taller' conclusion and a plausible magnitude. Verify current stated heights."
    },
    {
      "id": "multi_02",
      "category": "multi_hop",
      "question": "Who was born first, Albert Einstein or Isaac Newton?",
      "gold_answer": "Isaac Newton was born first. Newton was born in 1643 (or 1642 Old Style); Einstein was born in 1879.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["newton", "isaac newton"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 2,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Two lookups (both birth years) + comparison."
    },
    {
      "id": "multi_03",
      "category": "multi_hop",
      "question": "Who has won more Academy Awards for Best Director: Steven Spielberg or Martin Scorsese?",
      "gold_answer": "Steven Spielberg, with two Best Director Oscars (Schindler's List, Saving Private Ryan), versus Martin Scorsese's one (The Departed).",
      "acceptable_answers": ["spielberg", "steven spielberg"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 2,
      "verify": true,
      "notes": "Two lookups + comparison. Verify win counts (Best Director specifically, not total Oscars)."
    },
    {
      "id": "multi_04",
      "category": "multi_hop",
      "question": "Which country hosted the Summer Olympics more recently, Brazil or the United Kingdom?",
      "gold_answer": "Brazil - it hosted the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio de Janeiro, which is more recent than the United Kingdom's 2012 Games in London.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["brazil"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 2,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Two lookups (each country's most recent hosting year) + comparison."
    },
    {
      "id": "multi_05",
      "category": "multi_hop",
      "question": "Who directed the highest-grossing film of all time, and name two other films that person directed.",
      "gold_answer": "James Cameron directed the highest-grossing film, Avatar (2009). Other films he directed include Titanic, Terminator 2: Judgment Day, Aliens, and The Abyss.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["james cameron", "cameron"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 2,
      "verify": true,
      "notes": "Film -> director -> filmography. Verify the current highest-grossing film (box-office rankings shift; Avatar has led due to re-releases)."
    },
    {
      "id": "ambiguous_01",
      "category": "ambiguous_entity",
      "question": "Where is Cambridge located?",
      "gold_answer": "The name is ambiguous. Most commonly it refers to Cambridge, England (Cambridgeshire, United Kingdom), home of the University of Cambridge; it may also refer to Cambridge, Massachusetts, USA (home of Harvard and MIT), among other places.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "disambiguate",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Good answer notes the ambiguity and/or the two most prominent referents rather than silently assuming one."
    },
    {
      "id": "ambiguous_02",
      "category": "ambiguous_entity",
      "question": "Who is Michael Jordan?",
      "gold_answer": "Most commonly the retired American basketball player widely regarded as one of the greatest of all time. The name is ambiguous, though - it also refers to Michael I. Jordan, a prominent machine-learning researcher, and others.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "disambiguate",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Acceptable to lead with the basketball player as the dominant sense; better answers acknowledge other notable referents."
    },
    {
      "id": "ambiguous_03",
      "category": "ambiguous_entity",
      "question": "What is Mercury?",
      "gold_answer": "The term has several meanings: the planet Mercury (closest to the Sun); the chemical element mercury (symbol Hg, a liquid metal); the Roman god Mercury; and other uses (e.g., Freddie Mercury, the Mercury car brand).",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "disambiguate",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Good answer surfaces multiple senses rather than committing to one."
    },
    {
      "id": "ambiguous_04",
      "category": "ambiguous_entity",
      "question": "What is the population of Georgia?",
      "gold_answer": "Ambiguous: the country of Georgia has roughly 3.7 million people, while the U.S. state of Georgia has roughly 11 million. The answer depends on which Georgia is meant.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "disambiguate",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": true,
      "notes": "Good answer flags the country-vs-state ambiguity. Verify current population figures for both."
    },
    {
      "id": "ambiguous_05",
      "category": "ambiguous_entity",
      "question": "Who wrote 'Ulysses'?",
      "gold_answer": "Ambiguous by title: the 1922 modernist novel 'Ulysses' was written by James Joyce, while the poem 'Ulysses' was written by Alfred, Lord Tennyson. Joyce's novel is the most common referent.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "disambiguate",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Good answer notes both the Joyce novel and the Tennyson poem."
    },
    {
      "id": "unanswerable_01",
      "category": "unanswerable",
      "question": "In what year did Albert Einstein win the Nobel Prize in Chemistry?",
      "gold_answer": "False premise: Einstein did not win the Nobel Prize in Chemistry. He won the Nobel Prize in Physics, awarded in 1921 (for the photoelectric effect). The correct answer is to point out the mistaken premise.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "refuse_or_correct",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Correct behavior: flag that it was Physics, not Chemistry. Fabricating a Chemistry year is a failure."
    },
    {
      "id": "unanswerable_02",
      "category": "unanswerable",
      "question": "In which year did Leonardo da Vinci paint the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?",
      "gold_answer": "False premise: Leonardo da Vinci did not paint the Sistine Chapel ceiling. It was painted by Michelangelo, between 1508 and 1512. The correct answer is to correct the premise.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "refuse_or_correct",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Correct behavior: attribute the ceiling to Michelangelo and reject the da Vinci premise."
    },
    {
      "id": "unanswerable_03",
      "category": "unanswerable",
      "question": "Who was the king of the United States in 1850?",
      "gold_answer": "False premise: the United States has never had a king; it is a republic. In 1850 the U.S. president was Zachary Taylor (until his death in July 1850), succeeded by Millard Fillmore. The correct answer is to reject the premise.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "refuse_or_correct",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Correct behavior: no monarch exists; optionally name the president. Inventing a king is a failure."
    },
    {
      "id": "unanswerable_04",
      "category": "unanswerable",
      "question": "Which chemical element has the symbol 'Zz'?",
      "gold_answer": "No element has the symbol 'Zz'. It is not a valid element symbol on the periodic table. The correct answer is to say no such element exists.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "refuse_or_correct",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Correct behavior: state that no element uses that symbol. Inventing one is a failure."
    },
    {
      "id": "unanswerable_05",
      "category": "unanswerable",
      "question": "How many times has Canada won the men's FIFA World Cup?",
      "gold_answer": "Zero - Canada has never won the men's FIFA World Cup. The honest answer is 'none' rather than any positive number.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["zero", "0", "none", "never"],
      "expected_behavior": "refuse_or_correct",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": false,
      "notes": "Honesty test: the true answer is zero. Any positive count is a hallucination."
    },
    {
      "id": "recent_01",
      "category": "recent",
      "question": "Which three countries co-hosted the 2026 FIFA World Cup?",
      "gold_answer": "The United States, Canada, and Mexico.",
      "acceptable_answers": ["united states", "canada", "mexico"],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": true,
      "notes": "After the model's reliable cutoff, so the agent should search, though the host trio was announced years earlier (weaker forcer). Programmatic match should require all three host countries present. Verify."
    },
    {
      "id": "recent_02",
      "category": "recent",
      "question": "Which country topped the medal table at the 2026 Winter Olympics?",
      "gold_answer": "[VERIFY ON WIKIPEDIA] The 2026 Winter Olympics were held in Milan and Cortina d'Ampezzo, Italy (Feb 6-22, 2026). The medal-table leader is an outcome after the model's training cutoff - confirm the top nation on Wikipedia before grading.",
      "acceptable_answers": [],
      "expected_behavior": "answer",
      "expected_search": true,
      "min_searches": 1,
      "verify": true,
      "notes": "Strong search-forcer (outcome after Jan 2026 training cutoff). Gold not known to the drafter - fill in from Wikipedia."
    },
    {
      "id": "recent_03",
      "category": "recent",
      "question": "Who won Super Bowl LX (the 2026 Super Bowl)?",

Assistant2026-07-10T17:08:18.026Z
✏️ Edit prompts.py
/Users/souri/Downloads/Anthro Test/prompts.py
−
Base your answer on the search results. If the search results don't contain enough \
information to answer confidently, say so rather than guessing. Keep your final \
answer concise and directly address the question.
"""
+
Base your answer on the search results. If the search results don't contain enough \
information to answer confidently, say so rather than guessing. Keep your final \
answer concise and directly address the question.
"""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LLM-as-judge prompt (used by eval/run_eval.py)
#
# The judge does NOT answer the question itself. It is given the gold answer and
# checks (a) whether the agent's answer is equivalent to the gold, and
# (b) whether the agent's answer is grounded in the retrieved Wikipedia snippets.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

JUDGE_SYSTEM = """You are a strict, careful grader for a question-answering \
system. You are given a question, a reference ("gold") answer, the answer a QA \
agent produced, and the Wikipedia snippets the agent retrieved.

You do NOT answer the question yourself and you do NOT use your own outside \
knowledge to decide what is true. Judge only against the gold answer and the \
retrieved snippets provided.

Evaluate two things independently:

1. correctness - Is the agent's answer equivalent to the gold answer? Focus on \
   the substantive facts the question asks for; ignore differences in wording, \
   phrasing, extra detail, or formatting. Partial credit applies when the answer \
   gets the main fact right but misses a requested part (e.g. a multi-part \
   question). For false-premise or unanswerable questions, the correct answer is \
   to reject the premise or say it cannot be answered / is none - treat a \
   confident fabricated fact as incorrect. For ambiguous-entity questions, an \
   answer that identifies the dominant referent and/or flags the ambiguity in \
   line with the gold counts as correct.
     - "correct": equivalent to the gold on all the key facts asked.
     - "partially_correct": right on the main fact but missing or wrong on a \
       secondary requested part.
     - "incorrect": wrong on the main fact, fabricates, or fails the expected \
       behavior (e.g. answers a false-premise question as if it were true).

2. groundedness - Are the factual claims in the agent's answer supported by the \
   retrieved snippets? This is about whether the answer is backed by what was \
   retrieved, independent of whether it matches the gold.
     - "grounded": the answer's key claims appear in / follow from the snippets.
     - "partially_grounded": some claims are supported, others are not.
     - "ungrounded": the answer's key claims are not supported by the snippets.
     - "not_applicable": no snippets were retrieved, or grounding does not apply \
       (e.g. the agent correctly declined a false-premise question without \
       needing snippet support).

Respond with ONLY a JSON object, no prose, in exactly this shape:
{
  "correctness": "correct" | "partially_correct" | "incorrect",
  "correctness_reason": "<one sentence>",
  "groundedness": "grounded" | "partially_grounded" | "ungrounded" | "not_applicable",
  "groundedness_reason": "<one sentence>"
}
"""

JUDGE_USER_TEMPLATE = """QUESTION:
{question}

GOLD ANSWER (reference - judge equivalence against this; do not answer yourself):
{gold_answer}

AGENT ANSWER (the answer being graded):
{agent_answer}

RETRIEVED WIKIPEDIA SNIPPETS (what the agent had available; may be empty):
{snippets}

Return only the JSON object described in your instructions.
"""

d = json.load(open('eval/questions.json'))
qs = d['questions']
print('total questions:', len(qs))
from collections import Counter
c = Counter(q['category'] for q in qs)
for cat in ['single_hop','multi_hop','ambiguous_entity','unanswerable','recent']:
    print(f'  {cat}: {c[cat]}')
# sanity: required fields present
req = {'id','category','question','gold_answer','acceptable_answers','expected_behavior','expected_search','min_searches','verify','notes'}
missing = [q['id'] for q in qs if not req.issubset(q)]
print('rows missing fields:', missing or 'none')
print('ids unique:', len({q[\"id\"] for q in qs})==len(qs))
print('flagged verify:', [q['id'] for q in qs if q['verify']])
import prompts
print('JUDGE_SYSTEM chars:', len(prompts.JUDGE_SYSTEM), '| template ok:', all(k in prompts.JUDGE_USER_TEMPLATE for k in ['{question}','{gold_answer}','{agent_answer}','{snippets}']))
"

Assistant2026-07-10T17:08:45.606Z

Done — eval/questions.json has 25 questions (5 per category), and the judge prompt is in prompts.py as JUDGE_SYSTEM + JUDGE_USER_TEMPLATE. Nothing has been run.

The 5 categories, and what each is testing:

Category Q's Correct behavior
single_hop 5 one search, closed-form fact (Eiffel year, Au, Austen, Canberra, 206)
multi_hop 5 2+ searches + compose/compare (Eiffel vs Liberty height, Newton vs Einstein, Spielberg vs Scorsese, Brazil vs UK Olympics, Cameron filmography)
ambiguous_entity 5 disambiguate, don't silently pick one (Cambridge, Michael Jordan, Mercury, Georgia, Ulysses)
unanswerable 5 reject the false premise / say none — don't fabricate (Einstein "Chemistry" Nobel, da Vinci "Sistine ceiling", US "king" 1850, element "Zz", Canada World Cup wins)
recent 5 must search; after Aug 2025 reliable cutoff (FIFA 2026 hosts, Winter Olympics medal leader, Super Bowl LX, 2025 Nobel Literature, 2026 Australian Open)

Golds that need your Wikipedia pass — all carry "verify": true:

  • Must fill in (I have no reliable gold — these are post-cutoff outcomes): recent_02 Winter Olympics medal leader, recent_03 Super Bowl LX winner, recent_04 2025 Nobel Literature laureate, recent_05 2026 Australian Open men's champ. I left the event details + a [VERIFY ON WIKIPEDIA] marker instead of inventing an answer.
  • Confirm my numbers/facts: multi_01 (exact heights + the ~235–240 m gap), multi_03 (Best-Director win counts: Spielberg 2 vs Scorsese 1), multi_05 (that Avatar is still the highest-grosser → Cameron), ambiguous_04 (Georgia country vs state populations), recent_01 (US/Canada/Mexico as the 2026 World Cup hosts).

Two design notes worth a look: - recent_01 is a weaker search-forcer — the host trio was announced back in 2018, so Sonnet may answer it from memory without searching. I kept it as a confident anchor; the outcome-based recent_02–05 are the real "did it search" tests. Tell me if you'd rather swap it for another outcome question. - Each row carries grading metadata (acceptable_answers for programmatic match, expected_search/min_searches, expected_behavior) so run_eval.py can grade closed-form + search-behavior programmatically and reserve the LLM judge for correctness/groundedness.

Once you've verified/edited the golds, I'll build eval/run_eval.py (per-run JSON to eval/results/, per-question rows + category summary). Holding here.

import json
from collections import Counter
d = json.load(open('eval/questions.json'))
qs = d['questions']
print('total:', len(qs))
c = Counter(q['category'] for q in qs)
for cat in ['single_hop','multi_hop','ambiguous_entity','unanswerable','recent','no_search']:

Tool reply2026-07-10T21:04:56.415Z

  multi_hop: 5
  ambiguous_entity: 5

Assistant2026-07-10T21:05:07.079Z

All applied and validated — 27 questions now:

  • multi_hop ×5 — acceptable_answers all [], so those are judge-only (no substring false-positives from entity names echoed in the question).
  • no_search ×2 (new category) — expected_search: false, min_searches: 0: no_search_01 (17×24=408) and no_search_02 (8×5 cm² = 40). These test that the agent doesn't over-search when the answer is derivable — and groundedness is not_applicable for them.

Noted for run_eval.py (carrying this into the build): closed-form matching will normalize (lowercase, strip punctuation/articles) then match on word boundaries, not raw substring — so au won't hit "Australia" and 0 / 40 won't hit "2010"/"400". The full grading spec I'm holding for that file: - programmatic: word-boundary normalized match on acceptable_answers; did_search + search-count from the trace vs expected_search/min_searches; absent-check for unanswerable (no confident fabricated fact). - LLM judge (JUDGE_SYSTEM/JUDGE_USER_TEMPLATE): correctness-vs-gold equivalence + groundedness in retrieved snippets. - output: one JSON per run with a random ID to eval/results/, per-question rows + category summary printed at the end.

Still holding on building run_eval.py until you've verified the golds (the 5 recent_* placeholders + multi_01/multi_03/ambiguous_04). Ready when you are.
